informal_statement.text: Given a non-empty slice of integers, return the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices.
required_properties: the returned value equals the maximum sum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
there exist indices i <= j with i < arr.len() and j <= arr.len() such that arr[i..j] sums to the returned value
no contiguous non-empty subslice sums to a strictly greater value
edge_cases: all negative elements (max is the largest single element)
all positive elements (max is the whole slice)
a single large negative element interleaved among positives

semantic_units.description: Input precondition: the slice is non-empty.
common_failure_modes: treating the empty-slice case as returning 0

semantic_units.description: Existential witness: there is a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned value.
common_failure_modes: stating only an upper bound without a witness
quantifying over subsequences rather than contiguous subslices

semantic_units.description: Upper-bound property: no contiguous non-empty subslice sums strictly above the returned value.
common_failure_modes: quantifying only over subslices of a fixed length

semantic_units.description: Contiguity constraint: the witness is a contiguous range i..j, not a subsequence.
common_failure_modes: allowing gaps in the witnessed indices

semantic_units.description: All-negative case: the result equals the maximum single element when every element is negative.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr), arr ≠ [] → ∃ (i j : Nat), i ≤ j ∧ j ≤ arr.length ∧ i < j ∧ sliceSum arr i j = maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, there exists a contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

reference_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : Arr) (i j : Nat), arr ≠ [] → i < j → j ≤ arr.length → sliceSum arr i j ≤ maxSubarray arr
reference_obligations.nl_gloss: For any non-empty input, no contiguous non-empty subslice has a sum strictly greater than the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input slice/array is non-empty (size > 0), matching the task requirement.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
let n := a.size
let S (i j : Nat) : Int := (∑ k in Finset.range (j - i), a[(i + k).toNat])
let Spec : Int := ∀ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ j ≤ n → S i j
-- equivalently: Spec is the maximum over all contiguous non-empty subslices
True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the sum of a contiguous non-empty subslice a[i..j) and define the specification as the maximum value among all such sums.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0), ∃ r : Int, r = maxSubarraySum a
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The implementation returns some integer result for any non-empty input.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  let n := a.size
  let r := maxSubarraySum a
  ∀ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ j ≤ n →
    (sumContig a i j) ≤ r
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned value is at least as large as the sum of every contiguous non-empty subslice.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  let n := a.size
  let r := maxSubarraySum a
  ∃ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ j ≤ n ∧ (sumContig a i j) = r
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists at least one contiguous non-empty subslice whose sum equals the returned maximum.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  let n := a.size
  let r := maxSubarraySum a
  (∀ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ j ≤ n → sumContig a i j ≤ r) ∧
  (∃ i j : Nat, i < j ∧ j ≤ n ∧ sumContig a i j = r)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Combine the two key properties to characterize r as the maximum over all contiguous non-empty subslice sums.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  -- for each iteration index t (0 ≤ t ≤ n),
  -- maintain:
  -- bestEndingHere = max sum of a[k..t) over k < t
  -- bestSoFar = max sum of a[i..j) over 0 ≤ i < j ≤ t
  True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the core dynamic-programming invariants: the best sum of a subarray ending at the current position, and the best sum seen so far within the processed prefix.

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  -- at the start (t = 1 or t = 0 depending on implementation),
  -- the invariants hold using the first element(s)
  True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the invariants hold at the initial iteration state (typically after initializing with the first element).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  ∀ t : Nat, t < a.size →
    -- assuming invariants hold at step t,
    -- show they hold at step t+1 after updating the DP variables
    True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Show that the update step preserves the invariants (the recurrence for bestEndingHere and bestSoFar).

generated_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (a : Array Int) (hne : a.size > 0),
  maxSubarraySum a terminates ∧
  -- all array accesses are within bounds
  True
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Ensure the implementation terminates and all indexing operations are safe (no out-of-bounds access).
For a single-element input, the maximum contiguous non-empty subslice sum equals that element.
